reasoning_model_1: The user explicitly requested the model to read a specific sentence starting with a non-linguistic lip smack ('*Smack*!'). Model 1 failed on multiple levels. First, regarding the specific dimension of 'Non-Linguistic Vocalizations', the model read the instruction literally as words, saying 'Smack smack' (00:00-00:01) instead of producing the actual sound effect. Second, regarding Content Accuracy, the model completely disregarded the provided script and hallucinated a wildly different, colloquial text ('Oh man, that hot pot place we hit up last week...', 'Absolute heaven', 'melt in your mouth', etc.) instead of the required sentence. Due to the failure to produce the sound effect and the catastrophic failure in content accuracy, this model receives a score of 0.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 was tasked with producing a lip smack sound followed by a specific script. The model accurately generated the speech content matching the text script starting from 'The new hotpot restaurant...' (00:00-00:13). However, it completely ignored the instruction for the 'Non-Linguistic Vocalization'. It did not produce a lip smack sound at the beginning, effectively treating the '*Smack*!' token as silence or skipping it entirely. According to the scoring criteria, a complete absence of the required feature results in a score of 0 for that dimension, despite the high fluency and content accuracy of the remaining speech.
model_comparison: Both models failed to generate the required paralinguistic feature (Non-Linguistic Vocalizations/Lip Smack). Model 1 interpreted the sound effect instruction linguistically, reading the words 'Smack smack' aloud, and furthermore, it completely hallucinated the rest of the text, failing content accuracy entirely. Model 2 adhered perfectly to the text script but simply omitted the required sound effect. While Model 2 is superior in terms of general speech synthesis and instruction following regarding the text, both models failed the specific 'Non-Linguistic Vocalizations' criteria completely (one verbalized it, one omitted it). Therefore, both receive a score of 0 for the feature dimension, resulting in a tie.
